questions.questionText: The expected date of delivery (EDD) is calculated by Naegele's rule as:
questions.optionB: LMP + 9 months + 7 days (or LMP − 3 months + 7 days + 1 year)
questions.explanation: Naegele's rule: EDD = LMP + 9 months + 7 days (or first day of LMP − 3 months + 7 days + 1 year). Assumes: 28-day regular cycle, ovulation on day 14. Duration of pregnancy: 280 days (40 weeks) from LMP = 266 days from conception. First trimester: 0-12 weeks. Second: 13-28 weeks. Third: 29-40 weeks. Viability: 28 weeks (India), 24 weeks (developed countries). Term: 37-42 weeks. Reference: DC Dutta — Naegele's Rule.

questions.questionText: Quickening (first perception of fetal movements by the mother) occurs at:
questions.optionB: 18-20 weeks in primigravida, 16-18 weeks in multigravida
questions.explanation: Quickening: first maternal perception of fetal movements. Primigravida: 18-20 weeks (doesn't recognize movements earlier). Multigravida: 16-18 weeks (earlier recognition). Not a reliable method for dating. Fundal height milestones: 12 weeks (just palpable above symphysis), 20 weeks (umbilicus), 36 weeks (xiphisternum), 40 weeks (drops — lightening/engagement). USG: most accurate dating in first trimester (CRL at 7-12 weeks). Reference: DC Dutta — Quickening.

questions.questionText: The most accurate parameter for gestational age estimation in the first trimester is:
questions.optionB: Crown-rump length (CRL) measured by USG at 7-12 weeks — accuracy ±3-5 days
questions.explanation: CRL (Crown-Rump Length): most accurate USG parameter for dating in first trimester (7-12 weeks). Accuracy: ±3-5 days. Second trimester: BPD (biparietal diameter), FL (femur length), AC (abdominal circumference) — accuracy decreases with advancing gestation. First trimester dating: most reliable as growth is uniform. CRL of 7mm without cardiac activity → missed abortion. NT (nuchal translucency): measured at 11-13+6 weeks → increased in Down syndrome, cardiac defects. Reference: DC Dutta — USG Dating.
